title: AI Agents: concepts and relationships
description: Draft graph prototype for exploring AI agent concepts and selected relationships. This graph is not canonical taxonomy and does not promote Level 3 concepts.

nodes.label: AI Agent
nodes.description: A system that can pursue goals by reasoning, planning, using tools, and acting within an environment.

nodes.label: Autonomous Agents
nodes.description: Agents designed to operate with limited direct human intervention over a task or workflow.

nodes.label: Goals
nodes.description: Objectives, instructions, or desired outcomes that guide an agent's behavior.

nodes.label: Environment
nodes.description: The context, system, application, or world state that an agent observes and acts within.

nodes.label: Planning
nodes.description: The ability to form steps, strategies, or task decompositions before or during action.

nodes.label: Reasoning
nodes.description: The ability to infer, decide, compare options, or draw conclusions in support of agent behavior.

nodes.label: Memory
nodes.description: Stored context, history, facts, or state used to inform future agent decisions.

nodes.label: Tool Use
nodes.description: The ability for an agent to call external tools, services, APIs, or software functions.

nodes.label: Function Calling
nodes.description: A structured method for selecting and invoking functions or APIs from a model or agent workflow.

nodes.label: Action Execution
nodes.description: The act of carrying out planned steps, tool calls, or environment actions.

nodes.label: Monitoring
nodes.description: Observing agent behavior, outputs, state, and failures during operation.

nodes.label: Human Oversight
nodes.description: Human review, intervention, approval, or supervision applied to agent behavior.

nodes.label: Evaluation
nodes.description: Methods for measuring, testing, benchmarking, or auditing an agent's behavior and outcomes.

nodes.label: Guardrails
nodes.description: Constraints, checks, policies, or controls intended to keep agent behavior within acceptable bounds.

nodes.label: Multi-Agent Systems
nodes.description: Systems involving multiple agents that may cooperate, coordinate, compete, or communicate.

nodes.label: Agent Communication
nodes.description: Information exchange between agents, systems, or participants in a multi-agent setting.

nodes.label: Coordination and Negotiation
nodes.description: Processes for aligning actions, resolving conflicts, allocating work, or reaching agreements among agents.
